heading: Step 3: Click .
body:
This Atlas Resource Policy applies to all new clusters. For existing clusters that
do not comply with the Atlas Resource Policy, Atlas only allows users to make
changes to the cluster which brings the cluster into compliance.
For example, if `cluster0` runs on AWS (Amazon Web Services) and you apply an Atlas Resource Policy that blocks
clusters on AWS (Amazon Web Services), the only change users could make to `cluster0` while
it is non-compliant is switching the cloud provider to Azure (Microsoft Azure) or Google Cloud.To return a list of existing clusters that do not conform to your Atlas Resource Policy,
use the `GET`
[/orgs/\{ORG-ID}/nonCompliantResources](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-getorgnoncompliantresources) resource.Atlas generates an [Project Activity Feed](view-activity-feed) when
you create, update, or delete an Atlas Resource Policy. To help identify your activity feed events
and make tracking easier, we recommend creating multiple, simple Atlas Resource Policies.
For example, if you want to restrict the usage of a cloud provider and multiple regions,
consider creating one Atlas Resource Policy that restricts the cloud provider and another
Atlas Resource Policy that restricts the regions.**Tf:**Terraform> **Note:** 
>
> Additional Prerequisites
>
> Complete the following steps before continuing with this guide:
>
> - [Create an API key](atlas/configure-api-access) for your [paying
>   organization](atlas/billing) and store
>   your API keys as environment variables by running
>   the following commands:
>
>   ```bash
>   export MONGODB_ATLAS_PUBLIC_KEY="<your public key>"
>   export MONGODB_ATLAS_PRIVATE_KEY="<your private key>"
>   ```
>
> - Install the [Terraform CLI](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/install)To create an Atlas Resource Policy by using Terraform, configure the [MongoDB Atlas
Terraform Provider](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/mongodb/mongodbatlas/latest/docs),
then construct a policy by using [Cedar policy language](https://docs.cedarpolicy.com/) and specifying it in your
`main.tf` Terraform file.

codeBlocks.content:
export MONGODB_ATLAS_PUBLIC_KEY="<your public key>"
export MONGODB_ATLAS_PRIVATE_KEY="<your private key>"
